summary:
The patient requires a durable but flexible material that maintains proper bite alignment. This ensures both immediate function and prepares them adequately until more permanent treatment can be provided.
To summarize Agent1’s reasoning:

The need for a durable yet flexible material that preserves correct biting mechanics must be met during the period before full dental rehabilitation, as indicated by using a synthetic resin-based temporary bridge. Summary focuses on achieving equivalent performance characteristics typically associated with longer-lasting restorations despite being only provisional measures. 

This summary captures two main aspects highlighted—durability/flexibility and maintenance of appropriate occlusion—and emphasizes their importance relative to transitioning between stages of oral health management. It also reflects an understanding about balancing temporality versus necessity within such treatments.
